title: Badge UI Component
content:
The `Badge` component is a reusable UI element used to display status, labels, or categories. It provides a visually distinct way to highlight information. ## Key Exports * `Badge`: The main React component for rendering a badge. * `badgeVariants`: A `cva` (class-variance-authority) function for defining the visual styles of the badge based on its `variant`. ## Props The `Badge` component accepts the following props: * `variant` (optional, enum: `'default'`, `'secondary'`, `'destructive'`, `'outline'`): Determines the color scheme and appearance of the badge. Defaults to `'default'`. * `className` (optional, string): Allows for custom styling to be applied to the badge. * `...props`: Standard HTML attributes for a `div` element. ## Variants * **`default`**: Primary color scheme. * **`secondary`**: Secondary color scheme. * **`destructive`**: Color scheme indicating a destructive or warning state. * **`outline`**: Displays the badge with an outline and the foreground text color. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"; function MyComponent() { return (
Default Badge Secondary Badge Destructive Badge Outline Badge Custom Styled Badge
); } export default MyComponent; ```
